The strongest reusable ideas are lightweight project/global ergonomics and optional compaction reminders, but Mnemosyne’s stateless tool boundary, basename-derived identity, and string-only session save model should not replace `memory_context`, `generate-context.js`, or `session_bootstrap`. The exact strict validation command passed with `RESULT: PASSED` and `Summary: Errors: 0  Warnings: 0`, alongside the expected read-only warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`.
209495:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7903:60683:Define an external-memory adapter contract for Public that preserves server-minted session identity, cached continuity summaries, bootstrap/readiness payloads, and JSON-primary save semantics, while optionally adding Mnemosyne-style project/global aliases and compaction-time memory reminders on top of the existing transport plan.
209496:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7905:60694:Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Focus on what the external repo actually proves in `src/index.ts` and what the README describes about the Mnemosyne backend. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
209497:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7907:60702:The plugin exposes five memory tools: `memory_recall`, `memory_recall_global`, `memory_store`, `memory_store_global`, and `memory_delete`. Project scope is derived from the current directory name and auto-initialized during plugin load; the global collection is initialized lazily on first global write. The wrapper shells out to the `mnemosyne` binary via `Bun.spawn`, quotes search strings to avoid FTS syntax issues, and injects compact memory-usage guidance into the compaction prompt so agents retain memory awareness after context resets.
209498:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7909:60717:What this repo does **not** currently have is an OpenCode memory plugin that wraps a standalone local binary, a first-class project/global tool pair with Mnemosyne's lightweight ergonomics, or a memory-specific compaction hook that injects tool-usage guidance directly into the compaction prompt. Do not repeat the outdated claim that Public lacks hybrid BM25+vector memory retrieval; compare Mnemosyne against the current code, not stale assumptions.
209499:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7911:60729:9. Only after the wrapper analysis, read `external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/README.md`. Use it to extract hybrid-search claims, model choice, AGENTS.md guidance, offline-first positioning, and the stated project/global collection model. Distinguish clearly between wrapper-verified behavior and backend claims that are only documented here.
209500:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7913:60732:12. Resolve cross-phase boundaries explicitly. Do not redo phase `001` as generic FTS5 study or phase `003` as generic BM25 study. This phase owns wrapper-to-backend boundaries, vector-search implications, OpenCode plugin ergonomics, project/global scoping, AGENTS guidance, and memory-awareness compaction behavior.
209501:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7914:60739:    Research the external repository at /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/opencode-mnemosyne-main and identify concrete improvements for Code_Environment/Public, especially around hybrid search (BM25 + vector embeddings), OpenCode plugin architecture, memory scoping (project vs global), session compaction resilience, and offline-first memory design.
209503:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7924:60821:- project/global scoping model
209505:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7928:60837:Rank findings in this order: compaction-resilience leverage, fit with Public's current hybrid retrieval stack, clarity of wrapper-vs-backend boundary, usefulness of project/global scoping ergonomics, offline-first operational value, implementation cost, and clean differentiation from phases `001` and `003`.
209506:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7933:60872:- project/global scoping, AGENTS guidance, offline-first tradeoffs, and compaction survival are all covered directly

Set core=true for critical cross-project context that should always be available.",

Gives your AI coding agent memory that persists across sessions -- entirely offline, no cloud APIs.
209516:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7946:61272:| `memory_recall_global` | Search global memory for cross-project preferences |

209518:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7949:61288:| Core (global) | `global` (tagged `core`) | All projects + survives compaction |
209520:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7952:62141:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:309:| `evidence-gap-detector.ts` | -      | TypeScript | Z-score confidence check on RRF scores to detect low-confidence retrieval |

209522:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7954:63187:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/evidence-gap-detector.vitest.ts:2:// Transparent Reasoning Module: Z-score confidence check on RRF
209523:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7956:66237:   139	The two embedding models trade off quality against convenience. The local model (`all-MiniLM-L6-v2`) requires no API key and works offline, making it the right default for most projects. The Voyage Code 3 model produces 1024-dimensional vectors trained specifically on code, and consistently returns higher-quality results for complex queries on large codebases. Switching models requires a full reset and reindex because the vector dimensions are incompatible.

Gives your AI coding agent memory that persists across sessions -- entirely offline, no cloud APIs.

209532:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7968:66611:    57	| Core (project) | `<directory-name>` (tagged `core`) | Sessions + survives compaction |
209533:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7969:66612:    58	| Core (global) | `global` (tagged `core`) | All projects + survives compaction |
209537:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7974:66781:- **What it does**: Wrapper-confirmed, plus README-documented: the wrapper injects a short “search/store/delete/core/global” checklist during compaction, and the README recommends pasting a matching AGENTS block into projects. The published package exposes only the plugin wrapper surface, not separate validation, repair, or hygiene modules.
209538:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7976:66795:- **What it does**: Wrapper-confirmed: Mnemosyne exposes semantic recall for project/global stores, but nothing like trigger phrases, prompt-start matching, session-scoped activation, co-activation, or tiered content trimming. Public’s `memory_match_triggers` validates trusted session IDs, filters by scope, runs attention decay/co-activation, and limits by cognitive state.
209539:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7977:66809:- **What it does**: Wrapper-confirmed: the plugin silently auto-initializes project/global collections and returns a friendly install string when the binary is missing. README-documented: first use downloads a large local model. Public’s `memory_health` explicitly reports DB/model/vector/FTS/alias/orphan status and gates repair behind `confirmed:true`.
209540:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7978:66838:- Questions addressed: hygiene enforcement versus guidance, trigger-based recall, mutation safety, contradiction handling, operational health/repair, packaging boundary and external repo thinness, AGENTS guidance location, offline-first readiness cost.
209541:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7980:66861:- **What it does**: Wrapper-confirmed, plus README-documented: the wrapper injects a short “search/store/delete/core/global” checklist during compaction, and the README recommends pasting a matching AGENTS block into projects. The published package exposes only the plugin wrapper surface, not separate validation, repair, or hygiene modules.
209542:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7982:66875:- **What it does**: Wrapper-confirmed: Mnemosyne exposes semantic recall for project/global stores, but nothing like trigger phrases, prompt-start matching, session-scoped activation, co-activation, or tiered content trimming. Public’s `memory_match_triggers` validates trusted session IDs, filters by scope, runs attention decay/co-activation, and limits by cognitive state.
209543:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7983:66889:- **What it does**: Wrapper-confirmed: the plugin silently auto-initializes project/global collections and returns a friendly install string when the binary is missing. README-documented: first use downloads a large local model. Public’s `memory_health` explicitly reports DB/model/vector/FTS/alias/orphan status and gates repair behind `confirmed:true`.
209544:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7984:66918:- Questions addressed: hygiene enforcement versus guidance, trigger-based recall, mutation safety, contradiction handling, operational health/repair, packaging boundary and external repo thinness, AGENTS guidance location, offline-first readiness cost.
209545:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7986:66939:- **What it does**: Wrapper-confirmed: the plugin silently auto-initializes project/global collections and returns a friendly install string when the binary is missing. README-documented: first use downloads a large local model. Public’s `memory_health` explicitly reports DB/model/vector/FTS/alias/orphan status and gates repair behind `confirmed:true`.
209546:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7987:66968:- Questions addressed: hygiene enforcement versus guidance, trigger-based recall, mutation safety, contradiction handling, operational health/repair, packaging boundary and external repo thinness, AGENTS guidance location, offline-first readiness cost.
209547:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7990:66987:Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Focus on what the external repo actually proves in `src/index.ts` and what the README describes about the Mnemosyne backend. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
209548:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7992:66995:The plugin exposes five memory tools: `memory_recall`, `memory_recall_global`, `memory_store`, `memory_store_global`, and `memory_delete`. Project scope is derived from the current directory name and auto-initialized during plugin load; the global collection is initialized lazily on first global write. The wrapper shells out to the `mnemosyne` binary via `Bun.spawn`, quotes search strings to avoid FTS syntax issues, and injects compact memory-usage guidance into the compaction prompt so agents retain memory awareness after context resets.
209549:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7994:67010:What this repo does **not** currently have is an OpenCode memory plugin that wraps a standalone local binary, a first-class project/global tool pair with Mnemosyne's lightweight ergonomics, or a memory-specific compaction hook that injects tool-usage guidance directly into the compaction prompt. Do not repeat the outdated claim that Public lacks hybrid BM25+vector memory retrieval; compare Mnemosyne against the current code, not stale assumptions.
209550:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7996:67022:9. Only after the wrapper analysis, read `external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/README.md`. Use it to extract hybrid-search claims, model choice, AGENTS.md guidance, offline-first positioning, and the stated project/global collection model. Distinguish clearly between wrapper-verified behavior and backend claims that are only documented here.
209551:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7998:67025:12. Resolve cross-phase boundaries explicitly. Do not redo phase `001` as generic FTS5 study or phase `003` as generic BM25 study. This phase owns wrapper-to-backend boundaries, vector-search implications, OpenCode plugin ergonomics, project/global scoping, AGENTS guidance, and memory-awareness compaction behavior.
209552:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:7999:67032:    Research the external repository at /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/opencode-mnemosyne-main and identify concrete improvements for Code_Environment/Public, especially around hybrid search (BM25 + vector embeddings), OpenCode plugin architecture, memory scoping (project vs global), session compaction resilience, and offline-first memory design.
209554:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8009:67114:- project/global scoping model
209556:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8013:67130:Rank findings in this order: compaction-resilience leverage, fit with Public's current hybrid retrieval stack, clarity of wrapper-vs-backend boundary, usefulness of project/global scoping ergonomics, offline-first operational value, implementation cost, and clean differentiation from phases `001` and `003`.

209569:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8036:67573:| Core (project) | `<directory-name>` (tagged `core`) | Sessions + survives compaction |
209570:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8037:67574:| Core (global) | `global` (tagged `core`) | All projects + survives compaction |
209572:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8041:68620:The two embedding models trade off quality against convenience. The local model (`all-MiniLM-L6-v2`) requires no API key and works offline, making it the right default for most projects. The Voyage Code 3 model produces 1024-dimensional vectors trained specifically on code, and consistently returns higher-quality results for complex queries on large codebases. Switching models requires a full reset and reindex because the vector dimensions are incompatible.

209632:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8161:79527:I’ve got enough evidence for the main gap candidates, but I’m pulling the exact readiness/health lines before I lock the offline feature recommendation. That way the final answer can cleanly say “Public already diagnoses X, but it still lacks Y,” instead of over-claiming a gap.
209635:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8166:79859:- **What it does**: Wrapper-confirmed: Mnemosyne pushes an explicit post-compaction checklist into `output.context` telling the agent when to recall, store, delete, use `core`, and use global memory. Public’s transport/plugin path pushes a deduped continuity block, but that block is transport-only state, not a memory-operations playbook.
209636:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8168:79866:- **What it does**: Both: Mnemosyne exposes separate project and global tools, with auto-init for project memory and lazy-init for global memory. Public exposes one generalized memory surface with `specFolder`, `tenantId`, `userId`, `agentId`, and `sharedSpaceId`; meanwhile its `retrievalLevel: global` is community fallback, not a personal/global preference lane.
209639:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8171:79908:- Questions addressed: compaction-specific memory guidance, project-vs-global ergonomics, direct memory capture, write-time pinning, offline readiness, AGENTS guidance location, overlap with existing hybrid retrieval and community-search fallback.
209640:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8172:79909:- Questions answered: Public already owns the heavy hybrid retrieval, trigger recall, health, and governance story. The real missing surface area is ergonomic: a compaction memory action card, explicit scoped lanes, inline capture, write-time pinning, and proactive warmup. Mnemosyne’s most transferable value is its thin agent-facing contract, not its ranking backend. The exact strict validation command passed with `Summary: Errors: 0  Warnings: 0` and `RESULT: PASSED`, while also emitting the read-only warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`. No `AGENTS.md` file was present under the external checkout; that guidance exists only in the README.

Project scope is derived from the current directory name and auto-initialized during plugin load; the global collection is initialized lazily on first global write. The wrapper shells out to the `mnemosyne` binary via `Bun.spawn`, quotes search strings to avoid FTS syntax issues, and injects compact memory-usage guidance into the compaction prompt so agents retain memory awareness after context resets.
209653:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8193:80069:What this repo does **not** currently have is an OpenCode memory plugin that wraps a standalone local binary, a first-class project/global tool pair with Mnemosyne's lightweight ergonomics, or a memory-specific compaction hook that injects tool-usage guidance directly into the compaction prompt. Do not repeat the outdated claim that Public lacks hybrid BM25+vector memory retrieval; compare Mnemosyne against the current code, not stale assumptions.
209654:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8195:80081:9. Only after the wrapper analysis, read `external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/README.md`. Use it to extract hybrid-search claims, model choice, AGENTS.md guidance, offline-first positioning, and the stated project/global collection model. Distinguish clearly between wrapper-verified behavior and backend claims that are only documented here.
209655:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8197:80084:12. Resolve cross-phase boundaries explicitly. Do not redo phase `001` as generic FTS5 study or phase `003` as generic BM25 study. This phase owns wrapper-to-backend boundaries, vector-search implications, OpenCode plugin ergonomics, project/global scoping, AGENTS guidance, and memory-awareness compaction behavior.
209656:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8198:80091:    Research the external repository at /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/opencode-mnemosyne-main and identify concrete improvements for Code_Environment/Public, especially around hybrid search (BM25 + vector embeddings), OpenCode plugin architecture, memory scoping (project vs global), session compaction resilience, and offline-first memory design.
209658:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8208:80173:- project/global scoping model

209671:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8233:80632:| `memory_recall_global` | Search global memory for cross-project preferences |

209677:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8243:81603:170:          await log.info(`Storing global memory: ${args.content}`);
209678:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8244:81715:139:The two embedding models trade off quality against convenience. The local model (`all-MiniLM-L6-v2`) requires no API key and works offline, making it the right default for most projects. The Voyage Code 3 model produces 1024-dimensional vectors trained specifically on code, and consistently returns higher-quality results for complex queries on large codebases. Switching models requires a full reset and reindex because the vector dimensions are incompatible.

209695:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8271:84644:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/17--governance/05-constitutional-gate-enforcement-rule-pack.md:19:In practice, this pack gives the memory system a compact "do not forget these rules" layer. The core gate-enforcement file is intentionally lean: it does not restate every gate in full, but instead keeps the most important trigger phrases, a gate cross-reference, quick-reference tables, and edge-case rules for continuation validation and compaction recovery. The companion constitutional README explains why this works operationally: constitutional memories are pinned to the top of search results, do not decay, and are designed to surface even when the user query is only loosely related.
209698:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8279:84876:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/17--governance/220-constitutional-gate-enforcement-rule-pack.md:29:| 220 | Constitutional Gate-Enforcement Rule Pack | Verify the always-surface constitutional memory preserves gate triggers, cross-references, and continuation or compaction recovery behavior | `Validate the constitutional gate-enforcement rule pack. Capture the evidence needed to prove the constitutional memory encodes file-modification, continuation, compaction, completion, and memory-save trigger phrases; cross-references the governing gates; includes continuation-validation and compaction-recovery overlays; and that the constitutional tier documentation guarantees top-of-results, non-decaying visibility for this rule pack. Return a concise user-facing pass/fail verdict with the main reason.` | 1) Inspect `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/constitutional/gate-enforcement.md` frontmatter and capture the constitutional importance tier, content type, and trigger-phrase coverage 2) Inspect the body of `gate-enforcement.md` and confirm the gate cross-reference table, continuation-validation workflow, compaction-recovery guidance, and quick-reference behavior are present 3) Inspect `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/constitutional/README.md` and confirm it documents constitutional ranking, fixed similarity, no decay, permanence, and budget/verification rules 4) Cross-check the rule-pack references against `AGENTS.md` Section 2 to confirm the constitutional pack points back to the authoritative gate contract | `gate-enforcement.md` declares constitutional importance and decision-style trigger metadata; the body includes gate cross-reference, continuation validation, compaction recovery, and quick-reference guidance; `constitutional/README.md` documents top-ranked, non-decaying constitutional surfacing semantics | Frontmatter capture from `gate-enforcement.md` + notes/snippets for cross-reference and recovery sections + constitutional README evidence + cross-check notes against `AGENTS.md` Section 2 | PASS if the rule pack preserves the operational gate overlays and constitutional surfacing guarantees described in the catalog; FAIL if trigger coverage is incomplete, recovery guidance is absent, or constitutional visibility semantics are undocumented | Verify `gate-enforcement.md` frontmatter still includes constitutional tier and trigger phrases -> inspect the gate cross-reference and continuation/compaction sections for accidental removal -> confirm `constitutional/README.md` still documents fixed-priority surfacing semantics -> check `AGENTS.md` Section 2 remains the referenced authoritative source |

209718:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8353:86915:Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. 209719:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8355:86923:The plugin exposes five memory tools: `memory_recall`, `memory_recall_global`, `memory_store`, `memory_store_global`, and `memory_delete`. Project scope is derived from the current directory name and auto-initialized during plugin load; the global collection is initialized lazily on first global write. The wrapper shells out to the `mnemosyne` binary via `Bun.spawn`, quotes search strings to avoid FTS syntax issues, and injects compact memory-usage guidance into the compaction prompt so agents retain memory awareness after context resets.
209720:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8357:86938:What this repo does **not** currently have is an OpenCode memory plugin that wraps a standalone local binary, a first-class project/global tool pair with Mnemosyne's lightweight ergonomics, or a memory-specific compaction hook that injects tool-usage guidance directly into the compaction prompt. Do not repeat the outdated claim that Public lacks hybrid BM25+vector memory retrieval; compare Mnemosyne against the current code, not stale assumptions.

209738:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8394:87709:     3	OpenCode plugin for **local persistent memory** using [Mnemosyne](https://github.com/gandazgul/mnemosyne). Gives your AI coding agent memory that persists across sessions -- entirely offline, no cloud APIs.

209749:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8410:87831:58:| Core (global) | `global` (tagged `core`) | All projects + survives compaction |
209751:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8488:91953:   139	The two embedding models trade off quality against convenience. The local model (`all-MiniLM-L6-v2`) requires no API key and works offline, making it the right default for most projects. The Voyage Code 3 model produces 1024-dimensional vectors trained specifically on code, and consistently returns higher-quality results for complex queries on large codebases. Switching models requires a full reset and reindex because the vector dimensions are incompatible.

209754:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8531:93443:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne hard-codes a memory-usage instruction block into `experimental.session.compacting`, telling the agent when to recall, store, delete, use `core`, and use global memory. Public already has transport-level compaction notes and prime packages, and Claude/Gemini already inject explicit recovery instructions, but OpenCode transport is still marked `retrievalPolicyOwner: 'runtime'` and only ships continuity payloads, not explicit memory-action guidance.
209757:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8536:93502:- Questions addressed: compaction-hook leverage, API-surface vs handler reality, operational `core` vs pinning semantics, runtime facade layering, memory-vs-code retrieval boundaries, offline-first readiness tradeoffs
209758:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8537:93503:- Questions answered: The strongest paradigm shifts are not new ranking algorithms. They are API and runtime shifts: compaction should preserve memory behavior, retrieval posture must be explicit in the tool contract, pinning should be separated from governance tiers, OpenCode should get a curated facade over the governed MCP core, and memory retrieval should remain one plane in a federated routing stack. The exact validation command passed with `Summary: Errors: 0  Warnings: 0` and `RESULT: PASSED`, while still emitting `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`.

Public already has transport-level compaction notes and prime packages, and Claude/Gemini already inject explicit recovery instructions, but OpenCode transport is still marked `retrievalPolicyOwner: 'runtime'` and only ships continuity payloads, not explicit memory-action guidance.

They are API and runtime shifts: compaction should preserve memory behavior, retrieval posture must be explicit in the tool contract, pinning should be separated from governance tiers, OpenCode should get a curated facade over the governed MCP core, and memory retrieval should remain one plane in a federated routing stack. The exact validation command passed with `Summary: Errors: 0  Warnings: 0` and `RESULT: PASSED`, while still emitting `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`.
209768:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8555:93666:Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Focus on what the external repo actually proves in `src/index.ts` and what the README describes about the Mnemosyne backend. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
209769:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8557:93674:The plugin exposes five memory tools: `memory_recall`, `memory_recall_global`, `memory_store`, `memory_store_global`, and `memory_delete`. Project scope is derived from the current directory name and auto-initialized during plugin load; the global collection is initialized lazily on first global write. The wrapper shells out to the `mnemosyne` binary via `Bun.spawn`, quotes search strings to avoid FTS syntax issues, and injects compact memory-usage guidance into the compaction prompt so agents retain memory awareness after context resets.
209770:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8559:93689:What this repo does **not** currently have is an OpenCode memory plugin that wraps a standalone local binary, a first-class project/global tool pair with Mnemosyne's lightweight ergonomics, or a memory-specific compaction hook that injects tool-usage guidance directly into the compaction prompt. Do not repeat the outdated claim that Public lacks hybrid BM25+vector memory retrieval; compare Mnemosyne against the current code, not stale assumptions.
209771:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8561:93701:9. Only after the wrapper analysis, read `external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/README.md`. Use it to extract hybrid-search claims, model choice, AGENTS.md guidance, offline-first positioning, and the stated project/global collection model. Distinguish clearly between wrapper-verified behavior and backend claims that are only documented here.
209772:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8563:93704:12. Resolve cross-phase boundaries explicitly. Do not redo phase `001` as generic FTS5 study or phase `003` as generic BM25 study. This phase owns wrapper-to-backend boundaries, vector-search implications, OpenCode plugin ergonomics, project/global scoping, AGENTS guidance, and memory-awareness compaction behavior.
209773:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8564:93711:    Research the external repository at /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/opencode-mnemosyne-main and identify concrete improvements for Code_Environment/Public, especially around hybrid search (BM25 + vector embeddings), OpenCode plugin architecture, memory scoping (project vs global), session compaction resilience, and offline-first memory design.
209775:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8574:93793:- project/global scoping model

209806:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8651:98436:- **What it does**: the README recommends putting the same recall/store/delete/global/core rules in `AGENTS.md`, while the compaction hook re-injects nearly the same operating model after context resets.
209807:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8653:98444:- **What it does**: project scope is derived from the working directory basename, `global` is a separate write/read path, and `core` is expressed as a simple tag. The compaction text teaches those choices in human terms rather than schema terms.
209808:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8657:98460:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne’s wrapper is just a Bun-spawned CLI bridge plus hook text; the hybrid BM25/vector/RRF implementation is only README-documented here. Public already has hybrid retrieval, `retrievalLevel` local/global/auto, community fallback, and internal `is_pinned` ranking behavior, but those operational controls are not surfaced as clearly in the OpenCode-facing memory contract.
209809:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8660:98490:Prototype a narrowly scoped OpenCode memory-policy addendum in the existing transport path: keep `session_bootstrap` and shared payloads authoritative, but add a bounded compaction/startup behavior block that tells OpenCode when to use `memory_match_triggers`, when to call `memory_context(...resume...)`, and how to choose `memory_search` with `retrievalLevel: local/global/auto`. That would import Mnemosyne’s strongest idea without diluting Public’s governed core.

209814:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8673:98573:Prototype a narrowly scoped OpenCode memory-policy addendum in the existing transport path: keep `session_bootstrap` and shared payloads authoritative, but add a bounded compaction/startup behavior block that tells OpenCode when to use `memory_match_triggers`, when to call `memory_context(...resume...)`, and how to choose `memory_search` with `retrievalLevel: local/global/auto`. That would import Mnemosyne’s strongest idea without diluting Public’s governed core.
209817:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8677:98610:- Questions addressed: compaction-hook leverage, API-surface vs handler reality, operational `core` vs pinning semantics, runtime facade layering, memory-vs-code retrieval boundaries, offline-first readiness tradeoffs
209818:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8678:98611:- Questions answered: The strongest paradigm shifts are not new ranking algorithms. They are API and runtime shifts: compaction should preserve memory behavior, retrieval posture must be explicit in the tool contract, pinning should be separated from governance tiers, OpenCode should get a curated facade over the governed MCP core, and memory retrieval should remain one plane in a federated routing stack. The exact validation command passed with `Summary: Errors: 0  Warnings: 0` and `RESULT: PASSED`, while still emitting `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`.
209819:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8681:98710:Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Focus on what the external repo actually proves in `src/index.ts` and what the README describes about the Mnemosyne backend. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
209820:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8683:98718:The plugin exposes five memory tools: `memory_recall`, `memory_recall_global`, `memory_store`, `memory_store_global`, and `memory_delete`. Project scope is derived from the current directory name and auto-initialized during plugin load; the global collection is initialized lazily on first global write. The wrapper shells out to the `mnemosyne` binary via `Bun.spawn`, quotes search strings to avoid FTS syntax issues, and injects compact memory-usage guidance into the compaction prompt so agents retain memory awareness after context resets.
209821:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8685:98733:What this repo does **not** currently have is an OpenCode memory plugin that wraps a standalone local binary, a first-class project/global tool pair with Mnemosyne's lightweight ergonomics, or a memory-specific compaction hook that injects tool-usage guidance directly into the compaction prompt. Do not repeat the outdated claim that Public lacks hybrid BM25+vector memory retrieval; compare Mnemosyne against the current code, not stale assumptions.
209822:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8687:98745:9. Only after the wrapper analysis, read `external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/README.md`. Use it to extract hybrid-search claims, model choice, AGENTS.md guidance, offline-first positioning, and the stated project/global collection model. Distinguish clearly between wrapper-verified behavior and backend claims that are only documented here.
209823:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8689:98748:12. Resolve cross-phase boundaries explicitly. Do not redo phase `001` as generic FTS5 study or phase `003` as generic BM25 study. This phase owns wrapper-to-backend boundaries, vector-search implications, OpenCode plugin ergonomics, project/global scoping, AGENTS guidance, and memory-awareness compaction behavior.
209824:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8690:98755:    Research the external repository at /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/opencode-mnemosyne-main and identify concrete improvements for Code_Environment/Public, especially around hybrid search (BM25 + vector embeddings), OpenCode plugin architecture, memory scoping (project vs global), session compaction resilience, and offline-first memory design.
209826:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8700:98837:- project/global scoping model
209828:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8704:98853:Rank findings in this order: compaction-resilience leverage, fit with Public's current hybrid retrieval stack, clarity of wrapper-vs-backend boundary, usefulness of project/global scoping ergonomics, offline-first operational value, implementation cost, and clean differentiation from phases `001` and `003`.
209829:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8709:98888:- project/global scoping, AGENTS guidance, offline-first tradeoffs, and compaction survival are all covered directly

209834:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8719:99503:    73	Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. 209862:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8786:100118:   170	          await log.info(`Storing global memory: ${args.content}`);
209863:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8788:100209:     3	OpenCode plugin for **local persistent memory** using [Mnemosyne](https://github.com/gandazgul/mnemosyne). Gives your AI coding agent memory that persists across sessions -- entirely offline, no cloud APIs.
209865:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8791:100248:    42	| `memory_recall_global` | Search global memory for cross-project preferences |
209866:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8793:100263:    57	| Core (project) | `<directory-name>` (tagged `core`) | Sessions + survives compaction |
209867:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8794:100264:    58	| Core (global) | `global` (tagged `core`) | All projects + survives compaction |
209869:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8798:101033:   139	The two embedding models trade off quality against convenience. The local model (`all-MiniLM-L6-v2`) requires no API key and works offline, making it the right default for most projects. The Voyage Code 3 model produces 1024-dimensional vectors trained specifically on code, and consistently returns higher-quality results for complex queries on large codebases. Switching models requires a full reset and reindex because the vector dimensions are incompatible.
209870:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8810:102535:/bin/zsh -lc 'rg -n "snowflake-arctic|Reciprocal Rank Fusion|RRF|global collection|directory-name|core" /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md' in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
209871:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8811:102540:57:| Core (project) | `<directory-name>` (tagged `core`) | Sessions + survives compaction |
209872:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8812:102541:58:| Core (global) | `global` (tagged `core`) | All projects + survives compaction |
209874:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8815:102975:### Finding 2: Public’s memory plane already goes deeper than Mnemosyne’s project/global split through `retrievalLevel` and community fallback
209875:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8816:102984:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne’s README recommends AGENTS instructions and its compaction hook reminds the agent that memory tools exist. Public goes further: bootstrap, auto-prime, and session-prime all surface concrete recovery calls and tool-routing guidance before the user has to remember the architecture.
209876:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8817:102989:### Finding 4: Mnemosyne’s project/global model is a thin UX alias over collection naming, not a replacement for Public’s governed scope model
209877:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8818:102998:- **What it does**: Public’s handler and runtime hints clearly support local or global memory retrieval plus automatic community fallback, but the shared `memory_search` schema block does not expose a `retrievalLevel` input at all. Mnemosyne avoids this kind of split because its project/global distinction is visible directly in separate tools.
209879:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8821:103049:### Finding 2: Public’s memory plane already goes deeper than Mnemosyne’s project/global split through `retrievalLevel` and community fallback

Public goes further: bootstrap, auto-prime, and session-prime all surface concrete recovery calls and tool-routing guidance before the user has to remember the architecture.

Mnemosyne avoids this kind of split because its project/global distinction is visible directly in separate tools.

209887:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8829:103151:- **What it does**: Public’s handler and runtime hints clearly support local or global memory retrieval plus automatic community fallback, but the shared `memory_search` schema block does not expose a `retrievalLevel` input at all. Mnemosyne avoids this kind of split because its project/global distinction is visible directly in separate tools.
209889:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8832:103195:Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Focus on what the external repo actually proves in `src/index.ts` and what the README describes about the Mnemosyne backend. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
209890:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8834:103203:The plugin exposes five memory tools: `memory_recall`, `memory_recall_global`, `memory_store`, `memory_store_global`, and `memory_delete`. Project scope is derived from the current directory name and auto-initialized during plugin load; the global collection is initialized lazily on first global write. The wrapper shells out to the `mnemosyne` binary via `Bun.spawn`, quotes search strings to avoid FTS syntax issues, and injects compact memory-usage guidance into the compaction prompt so agents retain memory awareness after context resets.
209891:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8836:103218:What this repo does **not** currently have is an OpenCode memory plugin that wraps a standalone local binary, a first-class project/global tool pair with Mnemosyne's lightweight ergonomics, or a memory-specific compaction hook that injects tool-usage guidance directly into the compaction prompt. Do not repeat the outdated claim that Public lacks hybrid BM25+vector memory retrieval; compare Mnemosyne against the current code, not stale assumptions.
209892:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8838:103230:9. Only after the wrapper analysis, read `external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/README.md`. Use it to extract hybrid-search claims, model choice, AGENTS.md guidance, offline-first positioning, and the stated project/global collection model. Distinguish clearly between wrapper-verified behavior and backend claims that are only documented here.

Resolve cross-phase boundaries explicitly. Do not redo phase `001` as generic FTS5 study or phase `003` as generic BM25 study. This phase owns wrapper-to-backend boundaries, vector-search implications, OpenCode plugin ergonomics, project/global scoping, AGENTS guidance, and memory-awareness compaction behavior.

209915:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8957:114671:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne auto-runs `mnemosyne init` for project/global collections but never checks the subprocess exit code in those init paths; the README also says first use downloads about 500 MB of models. If init or model download fails, the wrapper does not surface a readiness state up front. Public instead validates provider config, runs integrity checks, refuses embedding-dimension mismatches, and emits explicit degraded or fatal startup guidance.
209916:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8959:114699:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne always injects its memory-usage instructions during `experimental.session.compacting`; it does not check whether the binary is missing, init failed, or the backend is unhealthy. Public’s compaction transport only injects when a transport plan is available, tracks `runtimeReady`, preserves `lastRuntimeError`, and exposes a status tool.

Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
209924:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:8974:114900:The plugin exposes five memory tools: `memory_recall`, `memory_recall_global`, `memory_store`, `memory_store_global`, and `memory_delete`. Project scope is derived from the current directory name and auto-initialized during plugin load; the global collection is initialized lazily on first global write. The wrapper shells out to the `mnemosyne` binary via `Bun.spawn`, quotes search strings to avoid FTS syntax issues, and injects compact memory-usage guidance into the compaction prompt so agents retain memory awareness after context resets.

209946:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9019:115742:| Core (global) | `global` (tagged `core`) | All projects + survives compaction |
209948:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9026:117620:The two embedding models trade off quality against convenience. The local model (`all-MiniLM-L6-v2`) requires no API key and works offline, making it the right default for most projects. The Voyage Code 3 model produces 1024-dimensional vectors trained specifically on code, and consistently returns higher-quality results for complex queries on large codebases. Switching models requires a full reset and reindex because the vector dimensions are incompatible.

209963:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9046:121676:I’ve got the evidence I need. The synthesis is going to center on five concrete transfers: compaction guidance, explicit global/project ergonomics, core-memory authoring shorthand, startup/AGENTS memory discipline, and a thin OpenCode-native facade. I’m doing one small memory note lookup so I can either cite prior context cleanly or state that this iteration was effectively source-first.
209965:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9051:121727:- **Why it matters for us**: Integration effort is medium-high because this is not just a UI nicety. A `core=true` alias would have to map to constitutional or critical semantics, audit history, cache invalidation, and save/update guardrails without weakening governance.
209966:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9052:121753:- Questions addressed: top-5 adoption candidates, compaction-survival fit, startup/AGENTS discipline reuse, project/global ergonomics, `core` mapping, thin-plugin feasibility, current Public overlap with hybrid retrieval and CocoIndex
209967:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9053:121754:- Questions answered: the best near-term transfer is a health-gated memory compaction block; startup/AGENTS guidance is also easy to absorb; project/global aliases and `core=true` are feasible but need wrapper-only treatment over existing governed surfaces; a native OpenCode memory plugin is viable only as a delegating facade. Validation command used: `bash .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/validate.sh "/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main" --strict`, which returned `Summary: Errors: 0  Warnings: 0` and `RESULT: PASSED`, while also emitting `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`.

Validation command used: `bash .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/validate.sh "/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main" --strict`, which returned `Summary: Errors: 0  Warnings: 0` and `RESULT: PASSED`, while also emitting `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`.
209978:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9067:121887:Inspect the real Mnemosyne backend next for how `core` is actually retrieved and how global collections are namespaced, then compare that to Public’s `opencode-transport` builder so the next iteration can decide whether to implement only compaction/startup guidance or also add a thin OpenCode memory facade.
209979:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9069:121898:Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Focus on what the external repo actually proves in `src/index.ts` and what the README describes about the Mnemosyne backend. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
209980:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9071:121906:The plugin exposes five memory tools: `memory_recall`, `memory_recall_global`, `memory_store`, `memory_store_global`, and `memory_delete`. Project scope is derived from the current directory name and auto-initialized during plugin load; the global collection is initialized lazily on first global write. The wrapper shells out to the `mnemosyne` binary via `Bun.spawn`, quotes search strings to avoid FTS syntax issues, and injects compact memory-usage guidance into the compaction prompt so agents retain memory awareness after context resets.
209981:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9073:121921:What this repo does **not** currently have is an OpenCode memory plugin that wraps a standalone local binary, a first-class project/global tool pair with Mnemosyne's lightweight ergonomics, or a memory-specific compaction hook that injects tool-usage guidance directly into the compaction prompt. Do not repeat the outdated claim that Public lacks hybrid BM25+vector memory retrieval; compare Mnemosyne against the current code, not stale assumptions.
209982:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9075:121933:9. Only after the wrapper analysis, read `external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/README.md`. Use it to extract hybrid-search claims, model choice, AGENTS.md guidance, offline-first positioning, and the stated project/global collection model. Distinguish clearly between wrapper-verified behavior and backend claims that are only documented here.
209983:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9077:121936:12. Resolve cross-phase boundaries explicitly. Do not redo phase `001` as generic FTS5 study or phase `003` as generic BM25 study. This phase owns wrapper-to-backend boundaries, vector-search implications, OpenCode plugin ergonomics, project/global scoping, AGENTS guidance, and memory-awareness compaction behavior.
209984:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9078:121943:    Research the external repository at /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/opencode-mnemosyne-main and identify concrete improvements for Code_Environment/Public, especially around hybrid search (BM25 + vector embeddings), OpenCode plugin architecture, memory scoping (project vs global), session compaction resilience, and offline-first memory design.
209986:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9088:122025:- project/global scoping model

209998:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9109:122554:     3	OpenCode plugin for **local persistent memory** using [Mnemosyne](https://github.com/gandazgul/mnemosyne). Gives your AI coding agent memory that persists across sessions -- entirely offline, no cloud APIs.
210000:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9112:122593:    42	| `memory_recall_global` | Search global memory for cross-project preferences |
210001:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9114:122608:    57	| Core (project) | `<directory-name>` (tagged `core`) | Sessions + survives compaction |
210002:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9115:122609:    58	| Core (global) | `global` (tagged `core`) | All projects + survives compaction |
210004:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9122:124120:The wrapper trace is clear now: project scope is just the directory basename, the CLI bridge is a direct `Bun.spawn` delegation layer, and the compaction feature is pure prompt injection. I’m pulling the Public governance and mutation paths next, because that’s where most adoption risk lives.
210008:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9188:125414:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne is a tiny OpenCode plugin that delegates everything to a local binary via argv-safe `Bun.spawn`. Public already has a richer MCP server plus OpenCode transport payloads for bootstrap/resume/compaction.
210009:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9189:125415:- **Why it matters for us**: The biggest risk is split truth. If we add a native plugin that reinterprets save/search/scope logic instead of delegating to MCP, we will create semantic drift, duplicate failure handling, and mismatched recovery behavior. The offline-first dependency model also adds first-run latency and missing-binary failure states that Public’s current MCP surface does not require.
210010:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9190:125436:- Questions addressed: adoption risk for compaction reminders, startup/AGENTS policy reuse, project/global ergonomic aliases, `core=true` mapping, thin plugin delegation, offline-first failure handling, and separation from CocoIndex/code-graph responsibilities
210011:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9191:125437:- Questions answered: the safest near-term adoption is still the compaction reminder pattern, but only as a deduped add-on to existing compaction transport; AGENTS guidance should be rewritten for governed saves instead of copied; project/global and `core=true` need wrapper-only prototypes because Public’s scope and constitutional semantics are stricter than Mnemosyne’s; a native OpenCode facade is viable only if it delegates to MCP and never becomes a second memory authority. 210047:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9254:126314:          await log.info(`Storing global memory: ${args.content}`);
210048:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9257:126378:OpenCode plugin for **local persistent memory** using [Mnemosyne](https://github.com/gandazgul/mnemosyne). Gives your AI coding agent memory that persists across sessions -- entirely offline, no cloud APIs.
210050:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9260:126417:| `memory_recall_global` | Search global memory for cross-project preferences |
210051:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9262:126432:| Core (project) | `<directory-name>` (tagged `core`) | Sessions + survives compaction |
210052:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9263:126433:| Core (global) | `global` (tagged `core`) | All projects + survives compaction |
210054:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9276:128498:   120	          "Search global memory for cross-project preferences, decisions and patterns.",
