210056:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9278:128518:   140	          "Store a project memory: a decision, preference, or important context. One concise concept per memory. Set core=true for critical context that should always be available in every session (use sparingly).",
210057:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9279:128540:   162	          "Store a cross-project memory: personal preferences, coding style, tool choices. Set core=true for critical cross-project context that should always be available.",
210058:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9280:128548:   170	          await log.info(`Storing global memory: ${args.content}`);
210059:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9282:128612:     3	OpenCode plugin for **local persistent memory** using [Mnemosyne](https://github.com/gandazgul/mnemosyne). Gives your AI coding agent memory that persists across sessions -- entirely offline, no cloud APIs.
210061:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9285:128651:    42	| `memory_recall_global` | Search global memory for cross-project preferences |
210062:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9287:128666:    57	| Core (project) | `<directory-name>` (tagged `core`) | Sessions + survives compaction |
210063:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9288:128667:    58	| Core (global) | `global` (tagged `core`) | All projects + survives compaction |
210065:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9291:130466:   139	The two embedding models trade off quality against convenience. The local model (`all-MiniLM-L6-v2`) requires no API key and works offline, making it the right default for most projects. The Voyage Code 3 model produces 1024-dimensional vectors trained specifically on code, and consistently returns higher-quality results for complex queries on large codebases. Switching models requires a full reset and reindex because the vector dimensions are incompatible.
210066:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9320:130836:Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Focus on what the external repo actually proves in `src/index.ts` and what the README describes about the Mnemosyne backend. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
210067:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9322:130844:The plugin exposes five memory tools: `memory_recall`, `memory_recall_global`, `memory_store`, `memory_store_global`, and `memory_delete`. Project scope is derived from the current directory name and auto-initialized during plugin load; the global collection is initialized lazily on first global write. The wrapper shells out to the `mnemosyne` binary via `Bun.spawn`, quotes search strings to avoid FTS syntax issues, and injects compact memory-usage guidance into the compaction prompt so agents retain memory awareness after context resets.
210068:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9324:130859:What this repo does **not** currently have is an OpenCode memory plugin that wraps a standalone local binary, a first-class project/global tool pair with Mnemosyne's lightweight ergonomics, or a memory-specific compaction hook that injects tool-usage guidance directly into the compaction prompt. Do not repeat the outdated claim that Public lacks hybrid BM25+vector memory retrieval; compare Mnemosyne against the current code, not stale assumptions.
210069:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9326:130871:9. Only after the wrapper analysis, read `external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/README.md`. Use it to extract hybrid-search claims, model choice, AGENTS.md guidance, offline-first positioning, and the stated project/global collection model. Distinguish clearly between wrapper-verified behavior and backend claims that are only documented here.
210070:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9328:130874:12. Resolve cross-phase boundaries explicitly. Do not redo phase `001` as generic FTS5 study or phase `003` as generic BM25 study. This phase owns wrapper-to-backend boundaries, vector-search implications, OpenCode plugin ergonomics, project/global scoping, AGENTS guidance, and memory-awareness compaction behavior.
210071:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9329:130881:    Research the external repository at /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/opencode-mnemosyne-main and identify concrete improvements for Code_Environment/Public, especially around hybrid search (BM25 + vector embeddings), OpenCode plugin architecture, memory scoping (project vs global), session compaction resilience, and offline-first memory design.
210073:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9339:130963:- project/global scoping model
210075:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9343:130979:Rank findings in this order: compaction-resilience leverage, fit with Public's current hybrid retrieval stack, clarity of wrapper-vs-backend boundary, usefulness of project/global scoping ergonomics, offline-first operational value, implementation cost, and clean differentiation from phases `001` and `003`.
210076:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9348:131014:- project/global scoping, AGENTS guidance, offline-first tradeoffs, and compaction survival are all covered directly
210077:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9353:131052:### Finding 3: Read-side `project/global` aliases are worth prototyping, but only as thin wrappers over existing retrieval scope
210078:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9354:131054:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne exposes separate project/global recall tools; Public already has `retrievalLevel: local|global|auto`, but that capability is less ergonomic than Mnemosyne’s named split.
210081:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9358:131110:- Questions addressed: compaction-survival leverage, governed mutation mismatch, project/global ergonomics, AGENTS/startup reuse, `core=true` semantics, OpenCode plugin layering, offline-first tradeoffs, current hybrid-retrieval overlap, CocoIndex separation.
210082:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9359:131111:- Questions answered: Phase A adopt now: add a deduped memory-tool reminder to the existing compaction transport, and rewrite startup/AGENTS wording around `session_bootstrap` plus governed save flows. Phase B prototype later: add read-only `project/global` recall aliases that compile down to current retrieval scope, with no write aliases. Phase C hold or reject: do not add a direct `core=true` shortcut, do not replace Public’s retrieval stack with Mnemosyne, and only pursue an OpenCode-native facade if it delegates to MCP instead of becoming a second authority.
210083:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9366:131146:### Finding 3: Read-side `project/global` aliases are worth prototyping, but only as thin wrappers over existing retrieval scope

210113:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9427:131889:    42	| `memory_recall_global` | Search global memory for cross-project preferences |
210114:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9429:131904:    57	| Core (project) | `<directory-name>` (tagged `core`) | Sessions + survives compaction |
210115:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9430:131905:    58	| Core (global) | `global` (tagged `core`) | All projects + survives compaction |
210120:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9436:131988:    28	- **What it does**: Mnemosyne is a tiny OpenCode plugin that delegates everything to a local binary via argv-safe `Bun.spawn`. Public already has a richer MCP server plus OpenCode transport payloads for bootstrap/resume/compaction.
210121:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9437:131989:    29	- **Why it matters for us**: The biggest risk is split truth. If we add a native plugin that reinterprets save/search/scope logic instead of delegating to MCP, we will create semantic drift, duplicate failure handling, and mismatched recovery behavior. The offline-first dependency model also adds first-run latency and missing-binary failure states that Public’s current MCP surface does not require.
210122:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9438:132010:    50	- Questions addressed: adoption risk for compaction reminders, startup/AGENTS policy reuse, project/global ergonomic aliases, `core=true` mapping, thin plugin delegation, offline-first failure handling, and separation from CocoIndex/code-graph responsibilities
210123:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9439:132011:    51	- Questions answered: the safest near-term adoption is still the compaction reminder pattern, but only as a deduped add-on to existing compaction transport; AGENTS guidance should be rewritten for governed saves instead of copied; project/global and `core=true` need wrapper-only prototypes because Public’s scope and constitutional semantics are stricter than Mnemosyne’s; a native OpenCode facade is viable only if it delegates to MCP and never becomes a second memory authority. Validation command run: `bash .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/validate.sh "/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main" --strict`, which returned `Summary: Errors: 0  Warnings: 0` and `RESULT: PASSED`, while also emitting `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`.

210174:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9549:133216:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:34:### Finding 4: Mnemosyne’s project/global model is a thin UX alias over collection naming, not a replacement for Public’s governed scope model
210177:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9569:134952:   139	The two embedding models trade off quality against convenience. The local model (`all-MiniLM-L6-v2`) requires no API key and works offline, making it the right default for most projects. The Voyage Code 3 model produces 1024-dimensional vectors trained specifically on code, and consistently returns higher-quality results for complex queries on large codebases. Switching models requires a full reset and reindex because the vector dimensions are incompatible.
210178:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9597:135543:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne repeats “recall early, save after decisions, delete contradictions” in AGENTS and compaction, but Public’s save/delete paths are governed and more constrained.
210179:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9598:135550:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne gives simple project/global tool pairs; Public already has richer runtime scoping via `retrievalLevel` and broader fallback behavior.
210182:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9601:135613:- Executive summary: 10 consolidated decisions. `adopt now`: 2. `prototype later`: 2. `NEW FEATURE`: 2. `reject`: 4. The highest-confidence transfers are compaction-time memory action guidance and rewritten startup policy; the highest-confidence rejections are basename-derived scope, `core=true`, and Mnemosyne-style mutation semantics.
210183:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9602:135615:- Questions addressed: hybrid contract vs wrapper proof, compaction resilience, AGENTS/startup reuse, project/global ergonomics, scope identity stability, inline save ergonomics, constitutional pinning semantics, mutation/audit model, OpenCode facade design, federated retrieval separation, offline readiness tradeoffs
210184:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9604:135624:Design two packet-ready follow-ons before any runtime edits: one for a deduped OpenCode compaction memory action card owned by transport, and one for a governed `memory_remember` / read-only `project|global` facade that delegates entirely to existing MCP scope and save authority.

210191:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9615:135734:Design two packet-ready follow-ons before any runtime edits: one for a deduped OpenCode compaction memory action card owned by transport, and one for a governed `memory_remember` / read-only `project|global` facade that delegates entirely to existing MCP scope and save authority.
210192:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9633:- **What it does**: Engram has explicit session objects, Mex has a truth-audit and repair loop, Modus has governance/proposal/readiness planes, and MemPalace has protocol/status surfaces. Mnemosyne only adds AGENTS guidance plus a compaction reminder; it does not add a richer operator-facing control plane.
210193:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9638:### Finding 3: Scope separation converges, but Mnemosyne’s project/global split is uniquely simple and uniquely weak
210194:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:9640:- **What it does**: All systems partition memory somehow: Engram by agent/admin and sessions, Mex by scaffold vs repair plane, Modus by separate planes, MemPalace by layers and fact plane. Mnemosyne is the simplest: basename-derived project collection, a single global collection, plus `core` tagging.

210261:### Finding 5: Build an explicit offline warmup and doctor flow for memory readiness

210263:- **What it does**: Both: Mnemosyne documents a binary prerequisite and one-time local model download, and its wrapper returns a clear missing-binary install message. Public already has `memory_health` and startup readiness logs, but it still relies on lazy first-use initialization and manual follow-up when embeddings are not ready.
210275:- [memory-crud-health.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts)

210284:- Questions addressed: compaction-specific memory guidance, project-vs-global ergonomics, direct memory capture, write-time pinning, offline readiness, AGENTS guidance location, overlap with existing hybrid retrieval and community-search fallback.
210285:- Questions answered: Public already owns the heavy hybrid retrieval, trigger recall, health, and governance story. The real missing surface area is ergonomic: a compaction memory action card, explicit scoped lanes, inline capture, write-time pinning, and proactive warmup. Mnemosyne’s most transferable value is its thin agent-facing contract, not its ranking backend. The exact strict validation command passed with `Summary: Errors: 0  Warnings: 0` and `RESULT: PASSED`, while also emitting the read-only warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`. No `AGENTS.md` file was present under the external checkout; that guidance exists only in the README.
210288:- What worked: Reading [external/src/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts) first, then tracing directly into Public’s [tool-schemas.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts), [opencode-transport.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/context/opencode-transport.ts), [memory-search.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts), and [memory-crud-health.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts) made the true gaps look like product-surface gaps rather than retrieval-engine gaps.
210292:Prototype a thin Public-side memory facade with three pieces together: `memory_remember` for inline capture, a `scope`/`pin` authoring contract for project-personal-always-on memories, and a compaction memory action card injected alongside the existing transport payload. That will test whether Mnemosyne’s best idea for us is not new ranking math, but a much thinner agent-facing memory UX.
210299:You are a research specialist in hybrid search architectures (BM25 + vector embeddings), OpenCode plugin design, memory scoping patterns, and session compaction resilience. Work like a systems analyst who can separate TypeScript wrapper behavior from backend README claims, trace plugin lifecycle and hook wiring precisely, and translate OpenCode Mnemosyne's concrete design into practical improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`.
210303:Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Focus on what the external repo actually proves in `src/index.ts` and what the README describes about the Mnemosyne backend. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
210309:OpenCode Mnemosyne is an OpenCode plugin that wraps the Go-based Mnemosyne local memory backend. The external repo is intentionally thin: `src/index.ts` contains the plugin entry point, CLI bridge, tool registration, scope initialization, and the `experimental.session.compacting` hook, while `README.md` describes the backend's hybrid retrieval model and intended agent workflow. The documented retrieval stack combines full-text search (SQLite FTS5 with BM25 ranking) and vector search (sqlite-vec cosine similarity using `snowflake-arctic-embed-m-v1.5`), then fuses both with Reciprocal Rank Fusion. All inference is local, the ML model is downloaded on first use (~500 MB), and no cloud APIs are involved.
210311:The plugin exposes five memory tools: `memory_recall`, `memory_recall_global`, `memory_store`, `memory_store_global`, and `memory_delete`. Project scope is derived from the current directory name and auto-initialized during plugin load; the global collection is initialized lazily on first global write. The wrapper shells out to the `mnemosyne` binary via `Bun.spawn`, quotes search strings to avoid FTS syntax issues, and injects compact memory-usage guidance into the compaction prompt so agents retain memory awareness after context resets.
210317:| 001 | Engram | MCP memory server (Go, SQLite+FTS5) | 004 (FTS5 search) | Focus tool profiles, session lifecycle, topic keys |
210319:| 003 | Modus Memory | FSRS spaced repetition + BM25 | 004 (BM25 search) | Focus FSRS decay, cross-referencing, query expansion |
210324:`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit Memory with a hybrid vector/BM25 memory retrieval pipeline in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts`, startup and resume flows in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts`, and CocoIndex for semantic code search through `.opencode/skill/mcp-coco-index/README.md`. It also already has a compaction-oriented transport plugin in `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js` that injects structural context during `experimental.session.compacting`.
210326:What this repo does **not** currently have is an OpenCode memory plugin that wraps a standalone local binary, a first-class project/global tool pair with Mnemosyne's lightweight ergonomics, or a memory-specific compaction hook that injects tool-usage guidance directly into the compaction prompt. Do not repeat the outdated claim that Public lacks hybrid BM25+vector memory retrieval; compare Mnemosyne against the current code, not stale assumptions.
210331:2. Start with the plugin entry point: read `external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/src/index.ts` from top to bottom before the README. Confirm how the OpenCode plugin is declared, how `ctx.directory` / `ctx.worktree` are resolved, how project names are normalized, and where collection initialization happens.
210334:5. Examine `memory_recall` and `memory_recall_global` in detail. Trace argument schemas, quoted search-string handling, project vs global flags, `--format plain`, and how the wrapper surfaces "No memories found" versus real backend output.
210335:6. Examine `memory_store` and `memory_store_global` in detail. Trace write semantics, lazy creation of the global collection, project collection auto-init, and what these choices imply for memory scoping, onboarding friction, and failure modes.
210337:8. Examine the `experimental.session.compacting` hook implementation in `src/index.ts` line by line. Treat this as the most novel feature of the phase: capture exactly what guidance is injected, when it runs, and how that compares with Public's structural compaction plugin in `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js`.
210338:9. Only after the wrapper analysis, read `external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/README.md`. Use it to extract hybrid-search claims, model choice, AGENTS.md guidance, offline-first positioning, and the stated project/global collection model. Distinguish clearly between wrapper-verified behavior and backend claims that are only documented here.
210340:11. Compare Mnemosyne directly against current `Code_Environment/Public` code: `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts` for existing hybrid retrieval, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts` for session bootstrap/resume behavior, `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js` for compaction injection, and `.opencode/skill/mcp-coco-index/README.md` for semantic code-search positioning.
210341:12. Resolve cross-phase boundaries explicitly. Do not redo phase `001` as generic FTS5 study or phase `003` as generic BM25 study. This phase owns wrapper-to-backend boundaries, vector-search implications, OpenCode plugin ergonomics, project/global scoping, AGENTS guidance, and memory-awareness compaction behavior.
210346:14. After validation passes, run deep research using this exact topic:
210348:    Research the external repository at /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/opencode-mnemosyne-main and identify concrete improvements for Code_Environment/Public, especially around hybrid search (BM25 + vector embeddings), OpenCode plugin architecture, memory scoping (project vs global), session compaction resilience, and offline-first memory design.
210359:3. How effective is Mnemosyne's project-vs-global scope split, and would a similar ergonomic separation improve Public's memory-save and recall flows without duplicating existing scoped retrieval features?
210360:4. What exactly does the compaction hook preserve, and how does prompt-level tool guidance differ from Public's current structural-context compaction transport?
210361:5. What operational value comes from the plugin auto-initializing the project collection and lazily initializing the global collection, and what failure or ambiguity risks come with deriving the project key from the directory basename?
210362:6. How should the README's AGENTS.md recommendation be interpreted as workflow guidance: is it merely usage advice, or does it reveal a reusable memory-discipline pattern for Public sessions?

210801:### Finding 1: Turn compaction continuity into a first-class benchmark

210804:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne proves that compaction is a memory-awareness seam; Public already measures bootstrap duration/completeness, but neither system makes post-compaction recovery correctness an explicit KPI.

210855:### Finding 7: Reject Mnemosyne’s shortcut authority model on Public’s primary surface
210856:- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:27-30](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts), [external/src/index.ts:140-145](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts), [tool-schemas.ts:88-92](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts), [tool-schemas.ts:291-299](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts), [scope-governance.ts:250-262](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/governance/scope-governance.ts)
210858:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne ties scope to directory basename and lets `core=true` behave like an always-on shortcut; Public already has governed scope and constitutional-tier semantics.
210859:- **Why it matters**: These shortcuts are ergonomic, but they are too weak for Public’s trust, scope, and governance requirements.
210864:### Finding 8: Reject re-platforming Public’s retrieval or mutation core around Mnemosyne
210865:- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:104-135](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts), [external/README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md), [memory-search.ts:771-809](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts), [hybrid-search.ts:1530-1590](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/hybrid-search.ts), [mcp-coco-index/README.md:85-143](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/mcp-coco-index/README.md), [archival-manager.ts:490-555](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/archival-manager.ts)
210867:- **What it does**: Mnemosyne exposes a thin search/delete/store contract, while Public already has a deeper hybrid pipeline, confidence truncation, derived indexes, soft archival, and a separate semantic-code-search role for CocoIndex.
210868:- **Why it matters**: The transfer value is wrapper ergonomics and compaction behavior, not ranking math, hard-delete semantics, or system fusion.
210875:- Final classification: `adopt now = 2`, `prototype later = 2`, `reject = 2`, `NEW FEATURE = 2`
210876:- Best immediate moves: add the compaction memory action card, and formalize Public’s existing validation/archival/health telemetry as the benchmark baseline.
210877:- Best near-term builds: compaction continuity benchmarking and an audited inline `memory_remember` path.
210878:- Strongest rejections: basename-derived scope, `core=true` as a primary-surface shortcut, and any attempt to replace Public’s retrieval/mutation core with Mnemosyne’s thinner wrapper model.
210883:Open two packet-ready follow-ons first: one `P1` packet for the transport-owned compaction memory action card plus compaction-continuity benchmark, and one `P1/P2` packet for an audited `memory_remember` intake path with an optional scoped `project|global` facade that delegates to existing governed scope and save authority.

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '740,860p' .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts && printf '\\n---\\n' && sed -n '1500,1610p' .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/hybrid-search.ts" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
 succeeded in 0ms:
    detectedIntent,
    minState: minState ?? '',
    rerank,
    applyLengthPenalty,
    sessionId,
    enableSessionBoost,
    enableCausalBoost,
    includeTrace,
  });

  let _evalChannelPayloads: EvalChannelPayload[] = [];
  const cacheKey = toolCache.generateCacheKey('memory_search', cacheArgs);
  const cacheEnabled = toolCache.isEnabled() && !bypassCache;
  const cachedPayload = cacheEnabled
    ? toolCache.get<SearchCachePayload>(cacheKey)
    : null;

  let responseToReturn: MCPResponse;
  let goalRefinementPayload: Record<string, unknown> | null = null;

  if (cachedPayload) {
    responseToReturn = buildSearchResponseFromPayload(cachedPayload, _searchStartTime, true);
  } else {
    // Wait for embedding model only on cache miss
    if (!isEmbeddingModelReady()) {
      const modelReady = await waitForEmbeddingModel(30000);
      if (!modelReady) {
        throw new Error('Embedding model not ready after 30s timeout. Try again later.');
      }
    }

    // V2 pipeline is the only path (legacy V1 removed from the runtime pipeline)
    const pipelineConfig: PipelineConfig = {
      query: effectiveQuery,
      concepts: hasValidConcepts ? concepts : undefined,
      searchType: (hasValidConcepts && concepts!.length >= 2)
        ? 'multi-concept'
        : 'hybrid',
      mode,
      limit,
      specFolder,
      tenantId: normalizedScope.tenantId,
      userId: normalizedScope.userId,
      agentId: normalizedScope.agentId,
      sharedSpaceId: normalizedScope.sharedSpaceId,
      tier,
      contextType,
      includeArchived,
      includeConstitutional,
      includeContent,
      anchors,
      qualityThreshold,
      minState: minState ?? '',
      applyStateLimits,
      useDecay,
      rerank,
      applyLengthPenalty,
      sessionId,
      enableDedup,
      enableSessionBoost,
      enableCausalBoost,
      trackAccess,
      detectedIntent,
      intentConfidence,
      intentWeights: toIntentWeightsConfig(intentWeights),
      artifactRouting: artifactRouting as unknown as PipelineConfig['artifactRouting'],
      trace,
    };

    const pipelineResult: PipelineResult = await executePipeline(pipelineConfig);
    let resultsForFormatting = pipelineResult.results as unknown as SessionAwareResult[];

    // Phase B T018/T019: Community search fallback — inject community members on weak results
    let communityFallbackApplied = false;
    const shouldRunCommunitySearch = (
      isDualRetrievalEnabled() &&
      isCommunitySearchFallbackEnabled() &&
      effectiveQuery.length > 0 &&
      (retrievalLevel === 'global' || retrievalLevel === 'auto')
    );
    if (shouldRunCommunitySearch) {
      const isWeakResult = resultsForFormatting.length === 0 ||
        (retrievalLevel === 'global') ||
        (resultsForFormatting.length < 3 && retrievalLevel === 'auto');
      if (isWeakResult) {
        try {
          const communityResults = searchCommunities(effectiveQuery, requireDb(), 5);
          if (communityResults.totalMemberIds.length > 0) {
            // Fetch the actual memory rows for community member IDs
            const memberIds = communityResults.totalMemberIds.slice(0, 20);
            const placeholders = memberIds.map(() => '?').join(', ');
            const db = requireDb();
            const memberRows = db.prepare(`
              SELECT id, title, similarity, content, file_path, importance_tier, context_type,
                     quality_score, created_at
              FROM memory_index
              WHERE id IN (${placeholders})
            `).all(...memberIds) as Array<Record<string, unknown> & { id: number }>;

            if (memberRows.length > 0) {
              // Mark community-sourced results and assign a base score
              const communityRows = memberRows.map((row) => ({
                ...row,
                similarity: typeof row.similarity === 'number' ? row.similarity : 0.5,
                score: 0.45,
                _communityFallback: true,
              }));
              // Merge: append community results not already in pipeline results
              const existingIds = new Set(resultsForFormatting.map((r) => (r as Record<string, unknown>).id as number));
              const newRows = communityRows.filter((r) => !existingIds.has(r.id));
              if (newRows.length > 0) {
                resultsForFormatting = [...resultsForFormatting, ...newRows as unknown as SessionAwareResult[]];
                communityFallbackApplied = true;
              }
            }
          }
        } catch (err: unknown) {
          const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
          console.warn(`[memory-search] Community search fallback failed (fail-open): ${msg}`);
        }
      }

---
      // Non-critical enrichment — co-activation failure does not affect core ranking
      console.warn('[hybrid-search] co-activation enrichment failed:', err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err));
    }
  }

  // Folder relevance / two-pass retrieval (SPECKIT_FOLDER_SCORING)
  if (db && isFolderScoringEnabled() && reranked.length > 0) {
    try {
      const numericIds = reranked
        .map(r => r.id)
        .filter((id): id is number => typeof id === 'number');

      if (numericIds.length > 0) {
        const folderMap = lookupFolders(db, numericIds);
        if (folderMap.size > 0) {
          const folderScores = computeFolderRelevanceScores(reranked, folderMap);
          const rawTopK = process.env.SPECKIT_FOLDER_TOP_K;
          const parsedTopK = rawTopK ? parseInt(rawTopK, 10) : NaN;
          const topK = Number.isFinite(parsedTopK) && parsedTopK > 0 ? parsedTopK : 5;

          const twoPhaseResults = twoPhaseRetrieval(reranked, folderScores, folderMap, topK);
          const postFolderResults = twoPhaseResults.length > 0 ? twoPhaseResults : reranked;
          reranked = enrichResultsWithFolderScores(postFolderResults, folderScores, folderMap) as HybridSearchResult[];
        }
      }
    } catch (_folderErr: unknown) {
      // Folder scoring is optional and must not break retrieval
    }
  }

  // -- Stage D: Confidence Truncation (SPECKIT_CONFIDENCE_TRUNCATION) --
  // Run after the ranking pipeline so later boosts/promotions can rescue
  // candidates before low-confidence tails are trimmed.
  if (!evaluationMode) {
    try {
      const truncationResult: TruncationResult = truncateByConfidence(
        reranked.map(r => ({ ...r, id: r.id, score: r.score })),
      );

      if (truncationResult.truncated) {
        reranked = truncationResult.results.map(r => r as HybridSearchResult);
        s3meta.truncation = {
          truncated: true,
          originalCount: truncationResult.originalCount,
          truncatedCount: truncationResult.truncatedCount,
          medianGap: truncationResult.medianGap,
          cutoffGap: truncationResult.cutoffGap,
          cutoffIndex: truncationResult.cutoffIndex,
          thresholdMultiplier: GAP_THRESHOLD_MULTIPLIER,
          minResultsGuaranteed: DEFAULT_MIN_RESULTS,
          featureFlagEnabled: isConfidenceTruncationEnabled(),
        };
      }
    } catch (err: unknown) {
      // Non-critical — truncation failure does not block pipeline
      console.warn('[hybrid-search] confidence truncation failed:', err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err));
    }
  }

  // Preserve routing and Stage 4 trace metadata as explicit result fields so downstream
  // Formatters can opt-in to provenance-rich envelopes without relying on
  // Non-enumerable array shadow properties.
  if (reranked.length > 0) {
    reranked = reranked.map((row): HybridSearchResult => {
      const existingTraceMetadata =
        typeof row.traceMetadata === 'object' && row.traceMetadata !== null && !Array.isArray(row.traceMetadata)
          ? row.traceMetadata
          : {};

      return {
        ...row,
        traceMetadata: {
          ...existingTraceMetadata,
          ...(s4shadowMeta !== undefined ? { stage4: s4shadowMeta } : {}),
          ...(s4attributionMeta !== undefined ? { attribution: s4attributionMeta } : {}),
          ...(degradationMeta !== undefined ? { degradation: degradationMeta } : {}),
          evaluationMode,
          // Wire queryComplexity from router classification into trace
          queryComplexity: routeResult.tier,
          // Wire confidence truncation metadata into per-result trace (036)
          ...(s3meta.truncation ? {
            confidenceTruncation: {
              truncated: s3meta.truncation.truncated,
              originalCount: s3meta.truncation.originalCount,
              truncatedCount: s3meta.truncation.truncatedCount,
              medianGap: s3meta.truncation.medianGap,
              cutoffGap: s3meta.truncation.cutoffGap,
              cutoffIndex: s3meta.truncation.cutoffIndex,
              thresholdMultiplier: s3meta.truncation.thresholdMultiplier,
              minResultsGuaranteed: s3meta.truncation.minResultsGuaranteed,
            },
          } : {}),
        },
      };
    });
  }

  if (isContextHeadersEnabled() && reranked.length > 0) {
    const descriptionCache = buildDescriptionTailMap();
    if (descriptionCache.size > 0) {
      reranked = reranked.map((row) => injectContextualTree(row, descriptionCache));
    }
  }

  let budgetTruncated = false;
  let budgetLimit: number | undefined;
  if (evaluationMode) {
    reranked = applyResultLimit(reranked, options.limit);
  } else {
    // Apply token budget truncation after trace/header enrichment so token
    // estimates reflect the actual returned payload shape.

210895	## Findings
210896	### Finding 1: Turn compaction continuity into a first-class benchmark
210897	- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:210-223](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts), [spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js:396-417](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js), [session-bootstrap.ts:194-217](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts)
210898	- **Evidence type**: both
210899	- **What it does**: Mnemosyne proves that compaction is a memory-awareness seam; Public already measures bootstrap duration/completeness, but neither system makes post-compaction recovery correctness an explicit KPI.
210900	- **Why it matters**: This is the highest-risk failure mode for long-running agents.
210901	- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
210902	- **Priority / Effort / Impact score**: `P1` / `2/5` / `5/5`
210903	- **Impact**: high

210905	### Finding 2: Add a transport-owned memory action card during compaction

210909	- **Why it matters**: This is the cleanest immediate transfer from Mnemosyne and does not require changing retrieval authority.

210914	### Finding 3: Use Public’s existing validation, archival, and health telemetry as the benchmark baseline

210951	- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:27-30](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts), [external/src/index.ts:140-145](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts), [tool-schemas.ts:88-92](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts), [tool-schemas.ts:291-299](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts), [scope-governance.ts:250-262](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/governance/scope-governance.ts)

210968	## Assessment
210969	- New information ratio: `0.14`
210970	- Final classification: `adopt now = 2`, `prototype later = 2`, `reject = 2`, `NEW FEATURE = 2`
210971	- Best immediate moves: add the compaction memory action card, and formalize Public’s existing validation/archival/health telemetry as the benchmark baseline.
210972	- Best near-term builds: compaction continuity benchmarking and an audited inline `memory_remember` path.
210973	- Strongest rejections: basename-derived scope, `core=true` as a primary-surface shortcut, and any attempt to replace Public’s retrieval/mutation core with Mnemosyne’s thinner wrapper model.
210974	- Validation: `bash .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/validate.sh "/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main" --strict` returned `RESULT: PASSED` with `Errors: 0  Warnings: 0`; the same run emitted the known read-only warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`.
210975	- This was a static-analysis synthesis only. The sandbox is read-only, so I did not update `research/research.md`, `checklist.md`, `implementation-summary.md`, or save memory artifacts in the phase folder.
210976	
210977	## Recommended Next Focus
210978	Open two packet-ready follow-ons first: one `P1` packet for the transport-owned compaction memory action card plus compaction-continuity benchmark, and one `P1/P2` packet for an audited `memory_remember` intake path with an optional scoped `project|global` facade that delegates to existing governed scope and save authority.

codex
The core synthesis is already in the phase report, and I’m now validating the dependency edges behind it: which pieces are truly prerequisite work, which are ergonomic follow-ons, and which ideas should stay explicitly rejected. I’m checking the external wrapper, the compaction transport, and the governed save/scope surfaces side by side so the Q1/Q2/Q3 sequence is defensible.

     1	import path from "node:path";
     2	import { type Plugin, tool } from "@opencode-ai/plugin";
     3	
     4	export const MnemosynePlugin: Plugin = async (ctx) => {
     5	  const { directory, worktree, client } = ctx;
     6	  const targetDir = directory || worktree || process.cwd();
     7	
     8	  const log = {
     9	    debug: (msg: string) =>
    10	      client.app
    11	        .log({ body: { service: "mnemosyne", level: "debug", message: msg } })
    12	        .catch(() => {}),
    13	    info: (msg: string) =>
    14	      client.app
    15	        .log({ body: { service: "mnemosyne", level: "info", message: msg } })
    16	        .catch(() => {}),
    17	    warn: (msg: string) =>
    18	      client.app
    19	        .log({ body: { service: "mnemosyne", level: "warn", message: msg } })
    20	        .catch(() => {}),
    21	    error: (msg: string) =>
    22	      client.app
    23	        .log({ body: { service: "mnemosyne", level: "error", message: msg } })
    24	        .catch(() => {}),
    25	  };
    26	
    27	  // Strip trailing slashes but keep the root slash if it's just "/"
    28	  let projectDir = targetDir.replace(/(.+?)\/+$/, "$1");
    29	  const projectRaw = path.basename(projectDir);
    30	  const project = projectRaw === "global" ? "default" : (projectRaw || "default");
    31	
    32	  await log.debug(`Plugin loaded for project: ${project} (dir: ${targetDir})`);
    33	
    34	  /**
    35	   * Run the mnemosyne CLI binary gracefully using Bun.spawn.
    36	   * Avoids shell interpolation entirely by passing args as array.
    37	   */
    38	  async function mnemosyne(...args: string[]): Promise<string> {
    39	    await log.debug(`Executing: mnemosyne ${args.join(" ")}`);
    40	    try {
    41	      // @ts-ignore - Bun is globally available in opencode environment
    42	      const proc = Bun.spawn(["mnemosyne", ...args], {
    43	        cwd: targetDir,
    44	        stdout: "pipe",
    45	        stderr: "pipe",
    46	      });
    47	
    48	      const [stdout, stderr, exitCode] = await Promise.all([
    49	        new Response(proc.stdout).text(),
    50	        new Response(proc.stderr).text(),
    51	        proc.exited,
    52	      ]);
    53	
    54	      if (exitCode !== 0) {
    55	        await log.error(`Execution failed (code ${exitCode}): ${stderr}`);
    56	        throw new Error(stderr.trim() || `mnemosyne ${args[0]} failed`);
    57	      }
    58	
    59	      // mnemosyne may write output to stderr (older versions), use whichever has content
    60	      const output = stdout || stderr;
    61	      await log.debug(`Execution successful. Output size: ${output.length}`);
    62	      return output;
    63	    }
    64	    catch (e: unknown) {
    65	      await log.error(`Execution error: ${e instanceof Error ? e.stack : String(e)}`);
    66	      const msg = e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e);
    67	      if (
    68	        msg.includes("not found") ||
    69	        msg.includes("ENOENT") ||
    70	        msg.includes("No such file")
    71	      ) {
    72	        return "Error: mnemosyne binary not found. Install it: https://github.com/gandazgul/mnemosyne#install";
    73	      }
    74	      throw e;
    75	    }
    76	  }
    77	
    78	  // Auto-init the project collection (idempotent).
    79	  try {
    80	    // @ts-ignore
    81	    await Bun.spawn(["mnemosyne", "init", "--name", project], {
    82	      cwd: targetDir,
    83	      stdout: "ignore", // Silence "collection already exists" logs
    84	      stderr: "pipe",   // Keep stderr for critical errors
    85	    }).exited;
    86	    await log.info(`Ensured collection exists: ${project}`);
    87	  }
    88	  catch (e) {
    89	    await log.warn(`Failed to auto-init collection: ${e}`);
    90	  }
    91	
    92	  return {
    93	    // ── Tools ──────────────────────────────────────────────
    94	
    95	    tool: {
    96	      memory_recall: tool({
    97	        description:
    98	          "Search project memory for relevant context, past decisions, and preferences. Use this at the start of conversations and whenever past context would help.",
    99	        args: {
   100	          query: tool.schema.string().describe("Semantic search query"),
   101	        },
   102	        async execute(args) {
   103	          await log.info(`Searching project memory for: ${args.query}`);
   104	          // Quote the query to prevent SQLite FTS errors with hyphens and special characters
   105	          const safeQuery = `"${args.query.replaceAll('"', '""')}"`;
   106	          const result = await mnemosyne(
   107	            "search",
   108	            "--name",
   109	            project,
   110	            "--format",
   111	            "plain",
   112	            safeQuery,
   113	          );
   114	          return result.trim() || "No memories found.";
   115	        },
   116	      }),
   117	
   118	      memory_recall_global: tool({
   119	        description:
   120	          "Search global memory for cross-project preferences, decisions and patterns.",
   121	        args: {
   122	          query: tool.schema.string().describe("Semantic search query"),
   123	        },
   124	        async execute(args) {
   125	          await log.info(`Searching global memory for: ${args.query}`);
   126	          const safeQuery = `"${args.query.replaceAll('"', '""')}"`;
   127	          const result = await mnemosyne(
   128	            "search",

   130	            "--format",
   131	            "plain",
   132	            safeQuery,
   133	          );
   134	          return result.trim() || "No global memories found.";
   135	        },
   136	      }),
   137	
   138	      memory_store: tool({
   139	        description:
   140	          "Store a project memory: a decision, preference, or important context. One concise concept per memory. Set core=true for critical context that should always be available in every session (use sparingly).",

   146	        },

   150	          if (args.core) {
   151	            cmdArgs.push("--tag", "core");
   152	          }

   154	          return (
   155	            await mnemosyne(...cmdArgs)
   156	          ).trim();
   157	        },
   158	      }),
   159	
   160	      memory_store_global: tool({
   161	        description:
   162	          "Store a cross-project memory: personal preferences, coding style, tool choices. Set core=true for critical cross-project context that should always be available.",
   163	        args: {
   164	          content: tool.schema.string().describe("Global memory to store"),
   165	          core: tool.schema.boolean().optional().describe(
   166	            "If true, this memory is always injected into context. Use sparingly."
   167	          ),
   168	        },
   169	        async execute(args) {
   170	          await log.info(`Storing global memory: ${args.content}`);
   171	          // Ensure the global collection exists.
   172	          try {
   173	            // @ts-ignore
   174	            await Bun.spawn(["mnemosyne", "init", "--global"], {
   175	              cwd: targetDir,
   176	              stdout: "ignore", // Silence "collection already exists" logs
   177	              stderr: "pipe",   // Keep stderr for critical errors
   178	            }).exited;
   179	            await log.info("Ensured global collection exists.");
   180	          }
   181	          catch (e) {
   182	            await log.warn(`Failed to auto-init global collection: ${e}`);
   183	          }
   184	          const cmdArgs = ["add", "--global"];
   185	          if (args.core) {
   186	            cmdArgs.push("--tag", "core");
   187	          }
   188	          cmdArgs.push(args.content);
   189	          return (await mnemosyne(...cmdArgs)).trim();
   190	        },
   191	      }),
   192	
   193	      memory_delete: tool({
   194	        description:
   195	          "Delete an outdated or incorrect memory by its document ID (shown in [brackets] in recall/list results).",
   196	        args: {
   197	          id: tool.schema.number().describe("Document ID to delete"),
   198	        },
   199	        async execute(args) {
   200	          await log.info(`Deleting memory document ID: ${args.id}`);
   201	          return (await mnemosyne("delete", String(args.id))).trim();
   202	        },
   203	      }),
   204	    },
   205	
   206	    // ── Hooks ──────────────────────────────────────────────
   207	
   208	    // Inject memory instructions into compaction so they survive
   209	    // context window resets.
   210	    "experimental.session.compacting": async (_input, output) => {
   211	      output.context.push(`## Persistent Memory (mnemosyne)
   212	
   213	You have persistent memory tools: memory_recall, memory_store, memory_delete,
   214	memory_recall_global, memory_store_global.
   215	
   216	When to use memory:
   217	- Search memory when past context would help answer the user's request.
   218	- Store concise summaries of important decisions, preferences, and patterns.
   219	- Delete outdated memories when new decisions contradict them.
   220	- Use **core** for facts that should always be in context (project architecture, key conventions, user preferences).
   221	- Use **global** variants for cross-project preferences (coding style, tool choices).
   222	- At the end of a session, store any relevant memories for future sessions.`);
   223	    },
   224	  };
   225	};

---
     1	# opencode-mnemosyne
     2	
     3	OpenCode plugin for **local persistent memory** using [Mnemosyne](https://github.com/gandazgul/mnemosyne). Gives your AI coding agent memory that persists across sessions -- entirely offline, no cloud APIs.
     4	
     5	This is the local/offline alternative to cloud-based memory plugins like opencode-supermemory.
     6	
     7	## Prerequisites
     8	
     9	Install the mnemosyne binary first:
    10	
    11	```bash
    12	# From source (requires Go 1.21+, GCC, Task)
    13	git clone https://github.com/gandazgul/mnemosyne.git
    14	cd mnemosyne
    15	task install
    16	```
    17	
    18	See the [mnemosyne README](https://github.com/gandazgul/mnemosyne#quick-start) for detailed setup instructions. On first use, mnemosyne will automatically download its ML models (~500 MB one-time).
    19	
    20	## Install
    21	
    22	Add to your `opencode.json`:
    23	
    24	```json
    25	{
    26	  "$schema": "https://opencode.ai/config.json",
    27	  "plugin": ["opencode-mnemosyne"]
    28	}
    29	```
    30	
    31	That's it. OpenCode will install the plugin automatically.
    32	
    33	## What it does
    34	
    35	### Tools
    36	
    37	The plugin registers five tools available to the AI agent:
    38	
    39	| Tool | Description |
    40	|------|-------------|
    41	| `memory_recall` | Search project memory for relevant context and past decisions |
    42	| `memory_recall_global` | Search global memory for cross-project preferences |
    43	| `memory_store` | Store a project-scoped memory (optionally as `core`) |
    44	| `memory_store_global` | Store a cross-project memory (optionally as `core`) |
    45	| `memory_delete` | Delete an outdated memory by its document ID |
    46	
    47	### Hooks
    48	
    49	- **`experimental.session.compacting`** -- Injects memory tool instructions into the compaction prompt so the agent retains awareness of its memory capabilities across context window resets.
    50	
    51	### Memory scoping
    52	
    53	| Scope | Collection | Persists across |
    54	|-------|-----------|-----------------|
    55	| Project | `<directory-name>` | Sessions in the same project |
    56	| Global | `global` | All projects |
    57	| Core (project) | `<directory-name>` (tagged `core`) | Sessions + survives compaction |
    58	| Core (global) | `global` (tagged `core`) | All projects + survives compaction |
    59	
    60	The project collection is auto-initialized when the plugin loads. The global collection is created on first use of `memory_store_global`.
    61	
    62	## AGENTS.md (recommended)
    63	
    64	For best results, add this to your project or global `AGENTS.md` so the agent uses memory proactively from the start of each session:
    65	
    66	```markdown
    67	## Memory (mnemosyne)
    68	
    69	- At the start of a session, use memory_recall and memory_recall_global to search for context
    70	  relevant to the user's first message.
    71	- After significant decisions, use memory_store to save a concise summary.
    72	- Delete contradicted memories with memory_delete before storing updated ones.
    73	- Use memory_recall_global / memory_store_global for cross-project preferences.
    74	- Mark critical, always-relevant context as core (core=true) — but use sparingly.
    75	- When you are done with a session, store any memories that you think are relevant
    76	  to the user and the project. This will help you recall important information in
    77	  future sessions.
    78	```
    79	
    80	## How it works
    81	
    82	Mnemosyne is a local document store with hybrid search:
    83	- **Full-text search** (SQLite FTS5, BM25 ranking)
    84	- **Vector search** (sqlite-vec, cosine similarity with snowflake-arctic-embed-m-v1.5)
    85	- **Reciprocal Rank Fusion** combines both for best results
    86	
    87	All ML inference runs locally via ONNX Runtime. Your memories never leave your machine.
    88	
    89	## Development
    90	
    91	This project uses standard Node.js tools: `npm` for package management and `tsc` (TypeScript compiler) for building.
    92	
    93	```bash
    94	# Install dependencies
    95	npm install
    96	
    97	# Build the project
    98	npm run build
    99	
   100	# Start the compiler in watch mode for development
   101	npm run dev
   102	
   103	# Run TypeScript checks
   104	npm run typecheck
   105	```
   106	
   107	## License
   108	
   109	MIT

---
   452	 * @param scope - Requested scope used for filtering.
   453	 * @param allowedSharedSpaceIds - Optional shared-space allowlist for the scope.
   454	 * @returns Predicate that returns `true` when a row remains visible.
   455	 */
   456	export function createScopeFilterPredicate<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(
   457	  scope: ScopeContext,
   458	  allowedSharedSpaceIds?: ReadonlySet<string>,
   459	): (row: T) => boolean {
   460	  const normalized = normalizeScopeContext(scope);
   461	  if (!isScopeEnforcementEnabled() && !hasScopeConstraints(normalized)) {
   462	    return () => true;
   463	  }
   464	  if (isScopeEnforcementEnabled() && !hasScopeConstraints(normalized)) {
   465	    // BUG-001 fix: Empty scope under enforcement means no access, not universal access.
   466	    return () => false;
   467	  }
   468	
   469	  return (row: T) => {
   470	    const rowSharedSpaceId = normalizeId(row.shared_space_id);
   471	    if (rowSharedSpaceId) {
   472	      if (normalized.sharedSpaceId && rowSharedSpaceId !== normalized.sharedSpaceId) {
   473	        return false;
   474	      }
   475	      if (allowedSharedSpaceIds && !allowedSharedSpaceIds.has(rowSharedSpaceId)) {
   476	        return false;
   477	      }
   478	    } else if (normalized.sharedSpaceId) {
   479	      return false;
   480	    }
   481	
   482	    // H8 FIX: When a row belongs to an allowed shared space, use membership as
   483	    // the access boundary — don't require exact actor/session match, which would
   484	    // block collaborator B from seeing collaborator A's shared memories.
   485	    if (rowSharedSpaceId && allowedSharedSpaceIds?.has(rowSharedSpaceId)) {
   486	      return matchesExactScope(row.tenant_id, normalized.tenantId);
   487	    }
   488	
   489	    return matchesExactScope(row.tenant_id, normalized.tenantId)
   490	      && matchesExactScope(row.user_id, normalized.userId)
   491	      && matchesExactScope(row.agent_id, normalized.agentId)
   492	      && matchesExactScope(row.session_id, normalized.sessionId);
   493	  };
   494	}
   495	
   496	/**
   497	 * Review governance audit rows and aggregate counts for a filtered governance window.
   498	 *
   499	 * @param database - Database connection that stores governance state.

---
    95	export function findSamePathExistingMemory(
    96	  database: BetterSqlite3.Database,
    97	  specFolder: string,
    98	  canonicalFilePath: string,
    99	  filePath: string,
   100	  scope: MemoryScopeMatch = {},
   101	): { id: number; title: string | null; content_hash?: string | null } | undefined {
   102	  const tenantId = normalizeScopeMatchValue(scope.tenantId);
   103	  const userId = normalizeScopeMatchValue(scope.userId);
   104	  const agentId = normalizeScopeMatchValue(scope.agentId);
   105	  const sessionId = normalizeScopeMatchValue(scope.sessionId);
   106	  const sharedSpaceId = normalizeScopeMatchValue(scope.sharedSpaceId);
   107	
   108	  return database.prepare(`
   109	    SELECT id, title, content_hash
   110	    FROM memory_index
   111	    WHERE spec_folder = ?
   112	      AND parent_id IS NULL
   113	      AND (canonical_file_path = ? OR file_path = ?)
   114	      AND ((? IS NULL AND tenant_id IS NULL) OR tenant_id = ?)
   115	      AND ((? IS NULL AND user_id IS NULL) OR user_id = ?)
   116	      AND ((? IS NULL AND agent_id IS NULL) OR agent_id = ?)
   117	      AND ((? IS NULL AND session_id IS NULL) OR session_id = ?)
   118	      AND ((? IS NULL AND shared_space_id IS NULL) OR shared_space_id = ?)
   119	    ORDER BY id DESC
   120	    LIMIT 1
   121	  `).get(
   122	    specFolder,
   123	    canonicalFilePath,
   124	    filePath,
   125	    tenantId,
   126	    tenantId,
   127	    userId,
   128	    userId,
   129	    agentId,
   130	    agentId,
   131	    sessionId,
   132	    sessionId,
   133	    sharedSpaceId,
   134	    sharedSpaceId,
   135	  ) as { id: number; title: string | null; content_hash?: string | null } | undefined;
   136	}

   138	/**
   139	 * Creates a memory row with metadata, optional BM25 entry, and save history.
   140	 * Returns the persisted memory id for downstream save handlers.

# Iteration 031: ADOPTION ROADMAP DRAFT

## Focus
ADOPTION ROADMAP DRAFT: Based on all 30 prior iterations, create a phased adoption roadmap with Q1/Q2/Q3 milestones. Include dependencies between adopted patterns.

## Findings
### Finding 1: Q1 starts with a transport-owned compaction memory action card
- **Source**: [SOURCE: research.md:210905-210912](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md#L210905), [SOURCE: external/src/index.ts:210-223](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L210), [SOURCE: spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js:396-417](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js#L396)
- **What it does**: Add Mnemosyne-style memory-tool guidance at the compaction seam, but keep it transport-owned like Public’s existing structural compaction hook.
- **Why it matters**: This is the cheapest high-leverage transfer and improves post-compaction agent behavior without changing retrieval or governance.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Dependencies**: none; this is the first roadmap milestone and unblocks meaningful compaction benchmarking in Q1.
- **Impact**: high
- **Source strength**: primary

### Finding 2: Q1 should formalize Public’s existing telemetry as the benchmark baseline
- **Source**: [SOURCE: research.md:210914-210921](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md#L210914), [SOURCE: memory-crud-health.ts:378-425](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts#L378), [SOURCE: memory-crud-stats.ts:126-175](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-stats.ts#L126)
- **What it does**: Treat current validation, archival, embedding/provider health, retry, and database-size surfaces as the measurement baseline for all follow-on memory experiments.
- **Why it matters**: Without a baseline, later roadmap items become feature work without proof of improvement.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Dependencies**: can run in parallel with Finding 1; both should finish before any Q2 prototype ships.
- **Impact**: high
- **Source strength**: primary

### Finding 3: Q1 closes by adding a compaction-continuity benchmark, not just a feature
- **Source**: [SOURCE: research.md:210896-210903](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md#L210896), [SOURCE: session-bootstrap.ts:194-217](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts#L194), [SOURCE: context-server.ts:782-789](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L782)
- **What it does**: Define a measurable continuity KPI around post-compaction recovery correctness and bootstrap completeness, instead of relying on prompt quality alone.
- **Why it matters**: The action card is useful, but the benchmark is what tells us whether compaction resilience actually improved.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Dependencies**: depends on Findings 1-2 so the benchmark measures the new compaction guidance against an explicit operational baseline.
- **Impact**: high
- **Source strength**: primary

### Finding 4: Q2 should add an audited inline `memory_remember` intake path
- **Source**: [SOURCE: research.md:210932-210939](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md#L210932), [SOURCE: external/src/index.ts:138-190](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L138), [SOURCE: tool-schemas.ts:217-220](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts#L217)
- **What it does**: Add a thin string-first memory intake surface that routes into Public’s existing governed save authority rather than bypassing it.
- **Why it matters**: This is the clearest product gap versus Mnemosyne and the most likely Q2 improvement to change day-to-day agent ergonomics.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Dependencies**: depends on Findings 2-3 so it ships with measurement, auditability, and compaction-aware evaluation from day one.
- **Impact**: high
- **Source strength**: primary

### Finding 5: Q2 to early Q3 can prototype a `project|global` facade only as an alias over governed scope
- **Source**: [SOURCE: research.md:210923-210930](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md#L210923), [SOURCE: external/src/index.ts:27-30](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L27), [SOURCE: scope-governance.ts:456-493](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/governance/scope-governance.ts#L456), [SOURCE: create-record.ts:95-135](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/save/create-record.ts#L95)
- **What it does**: Offer Mnemosyne-like ergonomic scoping labels while preserving Public’s tenant, actor, session, and shared-space enforcement model.
- **Why it matters**: The UX transfer is real, but the storage/governance model must remain Public-native.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Dependencies**: depends on Finding 4; the facade should wrap the audited inline intake path rather than becoming a separate write authority.
- **Impact**: medium
- **Source strength**: primary

### Finding 6: Q3 should add a deterministic `memory_warmup` or `memory_doctor` operator surface
- **Source**: [SOURCE: research.md:210941-210948](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md#L210941), [SOURCE: external/README.md:7-18](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L7), [SOURCE: external/README.md:80-87](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L80), [SOURCE: external/package.json:1-43](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/package.json#L1), [SOURCE: context-server.ts:1640-1715](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L1640)
- **What it does**: Expose a deterministic readiness path for embeddings, indexes, projections, and health checks before the first serious retrieval attempt.
- **Why it matters**: Mnemosyne’s offline-first story makes prerequisites explicit; Public has deeper internals, but its operator ergonomics are still more reactive than intentional.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Dependencies**: depends on Findings 2-3 so the warmup/doctor surface reports against known-good readiness and continuity metrics instead of inventing a new health model.
- **Impact**: medium
- **Source strength**: secondary

## Sources Consulted
- [research.md:210896-210978](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md#L210896)
- [external/src/index.ts:4-223](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L4)
- [external/package.json:1-43](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/package.json#L1)
- [external/README.md:33-87](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L33)
- [spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js:396-417](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js#L396)

- [context-server.ts:782-789](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L782)

- [scope-governance.ts:456-493](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/governance/scope-governance.ts#L456)
- [create-record.ts:95-135](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/save/create-record.ts#L95)
- [memory-crud-health.ts:378-425](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts#L378)
- [memory-crud-stats.ts:126-175](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-stats.ts#L126)
- Validation: `bash .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/validate.sh "/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main" --strict` -> `RESULT: PASSED`, `Errors: 0`, `Warnings: 0`, with the known read-only temp warning.

## Assessment
- New information ratio: `0.08`
- Questions addressed: phased sequencing, quarter boundaries, dependency ordering, adoption vs prototype split, and guardrail placement.
- Questions answered: Q1 should deliver compaction guidance plus measurement; Q2 should deliver audited inline ingest; Q2/Q3 should prototype ergonomic scope aliases; Q3 should improve operator readiness only after metrics exist.
- Novelty justification: This pass adds little new source discovery, but it converts the final recommendation set into an execution order that preserves Public’s governance model while maximizing measurable early wins.

## Ruled Out
- Mnemosyne-style basename-derived scope and `core=true` shortcut authority should not land on Public’s primary surface because Public’s save and retrieval paths require governed tenant/actor/session/shared-space enforcement.
- Re-platforming Public’s retrieval or mutation core around Mnemosyne should remain rejected; the transferable value is wrapper ergonomics and compaction behavior, not replacing Public’s hybrid pipeline or CocoIndex separation.

## Reflection
- What worked: using iteration 30 as the synthesis anchor and then re-checking only the source files that define compaction, scope, save authority, and health surfaces made the roadmap tighter and less speculative.
- What did not work: there was no new implementation work to inspect, so most value came from dependency synthesis rather than novel discovery.
- What I would do differently: for iteration 32, I would convert each Q1/Q2/Q3 milestone into packet-ready success criteria and explicit verification gates so the roadmap can be executed without re-arguing sequencing.

## Recommended Next Focus
Translate this roadmap into packet candidates: Q1 packet for compaction action card plus continuity benchmark, Q2 packet for audited `memory_remember`, and Q2/Q3 packet for optional `project|global` facade plus operator warmup/doctor validation plan. This was a read-only synthesis; I did not update the phase files or save memory artifacts in the phase folder.
